ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT HARLEY-DAVIDSON, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS HARLEY-DAVIDSON MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT HARLEY-DAVIDSON, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS HARLEY-DAVIDSON MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. RABENER WAS EXPOSED TO ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT BRAKES ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. JOHNSON PRECLUDES DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. MR. HEJLIK'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE ORIGINAL EQUIPMENT BRAKES ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT THAT MR. RABENER WAS EXPOSED TO CONTAINED ASBESTOS. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. RABENER'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING BRAKES ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING OR CONTRIBUTING TO MR. RABENER'S ASBESTOS-RELATED ILLNESS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. RABENER AND MR. HEJLIK ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michelle M. Clowser, Esq., of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Harley-Davidson, Inc., erroneously sued herein as Harley-Davidson Motor Company, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Michael Rabener

RULING:
DEFENDANT HARLEY-DAVIDSON, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS HARLEY-DAVIDSON MOTOR COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Harley-Davidson, Inc., erroneously sued herein as Harley-Davidson Motor Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Rabener was exposed to original equipment brakes attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Johnson precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Mr. Hejlik's deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether the original equipment brakes attributable to defendant that Mr. Rabener was exposed to contained asbestos. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Rabener's exposure to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Rabener's asbestos-related illness. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Rabener and Mr. Hejlik are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s Motion for Summary Judgment. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. The deposition testimony of Leslie Wilkes creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to Bendix brakes. The objections to Walker's testimony are overruled. Honeywell's motion for summary adjudication is also denied. Honeywell has not shifted the burden of production with respect to any of the issues for which it seeks summary adjudication. Honeywell fails to separate each cause of action and claim of damages in its separate statement of facts and further fails to provide sufficient evidence to satisfy its burden as to each issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR # 7235
